content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN where N≤10N \le 10N≤10. Consider any array AAA with length NNN where each element can either be 000, 111, or 222, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) as the number of extrema in AAA. You need to find the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over **all** possible arrays AAA.

**Note**:


In an array AAA, we consider AiA_iAi​ as an extremum if it is strictly greater than both of its neighbors (i.e. Ai>Ai−1A_i \gt A_{i - 1}Ai​>Ai−1​ **and** Ai>Ai+1A_i \gt A_{i + 1}Ai​>Ai+1​), or if it is strictly smaller than both of its neighbors (i.e. Ai<Ai−1A_i \lt A_{i - 1}Ai​<Ai−1​ **and** Ai<Ai+1A_i \lt A_{i + 1}Ai​<Ai+1​). Note that first and last elements are not counted as extremum.
Extrema is the plural of extremum.

Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. The test cases then follow.
The only line of each test case contains one integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all possible arrays AAA.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the sum of f(A) over all possible arrays A of length N whose elements are 0, 1 or 2.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 and N=2 there are no interior positions, so the sum is 0. For N=3 the formula gives (3-2)*10*3^{0}=10. For N=5 it gives (5-2)*10*3^{2}=3*10*9=270.

solution.approach:
For a fixed interior position i (2 ≤ i ≤ N‑1) the contribution of that position to the total sum equals the number of arrays where A_i is an extremum. By linearity of expectation we can sum contributions of each position independently. The three values (A_{i-1}, A_i, A_{i+1}) determine whether i is an extremum; the remaining N‑3 positions can be filled arbitrarily (3^{N‑3} ways). Enumerating all 3^3 = 27 triples of {0,1,2} gives 10 triples where the middle element is strictly larger than both neighbours (peak) or strictly smaller (valley). Hence for each interior i the count is 10·3^{N‑3}. There are (N‑2) interior positions, so the answer is:
    S(N) = 0                     if N < 3
    S(N) = (N‑2) * 10 * 3^{N‑3}  otherwise.
The formula is evaluated with 64‑bit integers (or Python's arbitrary precision).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * log N) for fast exponentiation, but with N ≤ 10 a simple loop O(N) is sufficient.
